title: Refresh process tied to FAOSTAT release cycle + drift detection
context: FAOSTAT revises and re-releases datasets periodically; a re-release can change the story told by a brief. Each derivative dataset pins its FAOSTAT version and release date, so drift detection means checking whether the source has been re-released since the pinned version. Because a revision can change figures materially, a refresh is a full rebuild plus re-review of the affected brief — not a silent update. This task documents and automates the drift-detection and refresh-scheduling process.
objective: Document and implement the refresh process: drift detection when FAOSTAT re-releases a pinned dataset version, and scheduling a rebuild + full re-review of the affected brief.
acceptanceCriteria: Drift detection: a documented and implemented mechanism to detect when a FAOSTAT dataset has been re-released vs. the pinned version (e.g. version/release-date check via FAOSTAT API or bulk-download manifest).
On drift detected: the process schedules a maintenance rebuild using the build harness and flags the affected brief as stale pending re-review; the brief is never silently updated.

Re-review requirement documented: a refresh triggers the full three-gate review (domain-expert, editorial, licence) before the updated brief/dataset is published, because revisions can change the story.
Process documented in a committed REFRESH.md or equivalent; the process is tied to the FAOSTAT release cycle (annual or per-domain re-release cadence).
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass for any tooling; commit is DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md — Risks & mitigations (source data revised/re-released risk)
FAOSTAT bulk download / API version/release metadata
output: A committed refresh-process document (REFRESH.md or equivalent) and any tooling for drift detection, covering rebuild scheduling and re-review requirements.
